title: Biodiversity intactness index — BII
code: BII(   gdx,   file = NULL,   level = "glo",   mode = "auto",   landClass = "sum",   spatialWeight = NULL,   adjusted = FALSE,   bii_coeff = NULL,   side_layers = NULL )

title: Biodiversity intactness index — BII
text: gdx GDX file file file name output written using write.magpie level level regional aggregation; "cell" (magpie cluster level), "reg" (regional), "glo" (global), "regglo" (regional global), "iso" (country level), "grid" (0.5 degree grid cell level). mode "auto" (default), "from_grid", "MAgPIE" "postprocessing". "MAgPIE" reports BV based values MAgPIE biodiversity module. "postprocessing" calculates BV based land information MAgPIE (versions biodiversity module available yet). "auto" uses "MAgPIE" available falls back "postprocessing" otherwise. "from_grid" calculates BII values BII output returns aggregated values aggregation level specified. landClass "" returns average BII values land classes ov_bv, "sum" returns weighted BII land classes ov44_bv_weighted. spatialWeight Spatial weight aggregating BII values. relevant mode "from_grid", adjusted TRUE, level either "grid" "iso". adjusted "TRUE", function returns adjusted BII values (results adjusted primary secondary land). bii_coeff file containing BII coefficients. needed mode = "postprocessing". NULL tries automatically detected file. side_layers file containing LUH2 side layers. NULL tries automatically detected file.

title: emisSOC — emisSOC
text: CO2 emissions MAgPIE object

title: emisSOC — emisSOC
text: function uses structural decomposition approach (Shapley decomposition) attribute soil carbon emissions different drivers interactions. framework based counterfactual analysis possible combinations driver states evaluated following inclusion-exclusion principle. four drivers, total emission decomposed : totEmissions = main_effects + first_order_interactions +                second_order_interactions + third_order_interactions + residual creates 16 counterfactual scenarios (2^4) isolate individual combined effects. Main effects capture changes one driver varies others stay constant. Interaction terms capture synergies driver combinations amplify dampen effects beyond simple addition. Drivers included: - Climate (C): Changes reference soil carbon density - Land-use (LU): Transitions land types - Management (M): Changes crop type shares, fallow, treecover   * Can split treecover vs management attribution - Soil Carbon Management (SCM): Enhanced soil carbon practices Legacy effects incorporated using exponential decay (85 100 years. Interaction terms proportionally attributed back main drivers final output.

title: emisSOC — emisSOC
text: Kristine Karstens

title: kayaFractions — kayaFractions
text: data MAgPIE object target variable variables calculate drivers. Needs target variable first column data dimension, variables calculate drivers following columns. .e. getItems(data, dim = 3) return c(t, d1, d2, ..., dn). decomposition calculated multiple scenarios, data object can scenario dimension 3.2. driverNames Names drivers data object. NULL, names drivers set based names variables d1, d2, ..., dn data object (.e. "d2/d1", "d3/d2", ..., "t/dn"). Name target variable always kept provided data object. Default NULL. fixTimeSteps Logical. consistent decomposition, time steps data object need equal length. fixTimeSteps TRUE, function check time steps equal length , interpolate data linearly equal time steps. fixTimeSteps FALSE, function throw warning time steps equal length. Default TRUE.

title: kayaLaspeyres — kayaLaspeyres
text: Calculates Laspeyres decomposition changes target variable t drivers based kaya-like identity. kaya-like identity needs form t = d1 * d2/d1 * d3/d2 * ... * dn/dn-1 * t/dn, d1, d2/d1, ..., dn/dn-1, t/dn drivers change target variable t. Laspeyres decomposition calculates additive decomposition change time target variable based changes drivers (formula laspeyres decomposition see comment .laspeyresDriver). function returns change target variable decomposition change drivers.

Needs target variable first column data dimension, variables calculate drivers following columns. .e. getItems(data, dim = 3) return c(t, d1, d2, ..., dn). decomposition calculated multiple scenarios, data object can scenario dimension 3.2. driverNames Names drivers data object. NULL, names drivers set based names variables d1, d2, ..., dn data object (.e. "d2/d1", "d3/d2", ..., "t/dn"). Name target variable always kept provided data object. Default NULL. type Type output. "relative" returns relative change target variable time step next decomposition drivers, "absolute" returns absolute change time decomposition. Default "absolute". fixTimeSteps Logical. consistent decomposition, time steps data object need equal length. fixTimeSteps TRUE, function check time steps equal length , interpolate data linearily equal time steps. fixTimeSteps FALSE, function throw warning time steps equal length. title: reportProcessingResiduesForestry — reportProcessingResiduesForestry
text: Reports processing residues timber production available energy use. Processing residues sawmill byproducts (sawdust, bark, slabs, offcuts) generated conversion industrial roundwood finished products. currently tracked variable MAgPIE's GAMS code represent additional biomass supply REMIND's bioenergy sector. Note: Logging residues (branches, tops) tracked v73_prod_residues included already feed MAgPIE's woodfuel supply equation (q73_prod_woodfuel) double-counted reported separately REMIND.

title: reportProcessingResiduesForestry — reportProcessingResiduesForestry
text: gdx GDX file level Level regional aggregation ("regglo" default)

title: reportProcessingResiduesForestry — reportProcessingResiduesForestry
text: MAgPIE object processing residues PJ/yr

what: Full mass balance of timber harvest
title: reportProcessingResiduesForestry — reportProcessingResiduesForestry
text: 1 hectare forest harvested, total biomass removed : Stem biomass = im_growing_stock (tDM/ha) Logging residues (branches, tops) = 15% stem, tracked v73_prod_residues Total removed = stem x 1.15 model splits stem production industrial roundwood (vm_prod("wood")) woodfuel (vm_prod("woodfuel")). industrial roundwood fraction enters processing facilities generates processing residues. Woodfuel burned directly. Logging residues already feed MAgPIE's woodfuel supply (reported ).
code: 1 hectare harvested -> total biomass = stem x 1.15 | +-- Stem (100% = vm_prod, split between "wood" and "woodfuel" by the model) |   If allocated to industrial roundwood (vm_prod("wood")) -> processing facilities: |   | |   +-- Sawnwood pathway (~57% of IR): |   |   +-- 50% finished sawnwood |   |   +-- 43% processing residues (sawdust, slabs, bark, offcuts) -> THIS FUNCTION |   |   +--  7% losses |   | |   +-- Pulpwood pathway (~35% of IR): |   |   +-- 95-100% utilized (black liquor burned internally at pulp mill) |   |   +-- 0-5% solid residues available externally |   | |   +-- Other industrial roundwood (~8% of IR): |       +-- Assumed similar to sawnwood (43% residue rate) | +-- Logging residues (15% of stem = branches, tops)     -> Already in MAgPIE woodfuel via v73_prod_residues (NOT reported here)

title: reportProcessingResiduesForestry — reportProcessingResiduesForestry
text: rate derived FAO product composition industrial roundwood (f73_prod_specific_timber, stable shares across 1965-2015) product-specific recovery rates: Production-weighted rate: 0.57 x 0.43 + 0.35 x 0.025 + 0.08 x 0.43 = 0.29 Rounded 0.30 (stable 30-35% across 1965-2015 FAO data).

what: Why only industrial roundwood, not woodfuel
title: reportProcessingResiduesForestry — reportProcessingResiduesForestry
text: Processing residues computed industrial roundwood supply (ov_supply("wood")) . Woodfuel (ov_supply("woodfuel")) excluded burned directly energy — pass sawmills pulp mills therefore generates processing residues.

what: Why all processing residues go to energy
title: reportProcessingResiduesForestry — reportProcessingResiduesForestry
text: reality, processing residues used pulp particleboard. However, MAgPIE models wood products aggregate "industrial roundwood" (kforestry = wood). downstream split sawnwood, pulp, panels modeled — competing use processing residues within MAgPIE. Therefore, processing residues assumed available energy use.

title: reportProcessingResiduesForestry — reportProcessingResiduesForestry
text: calcConstructionWoodDemand mrcommons applies factor 2 construction wood demand, reflecting 50% sawmill recovery rate (Churkina et al. 2020). means half harvested roundwood construction becomes processing waste. residues clean, uniform offcuts high energy recovery potential. Reported separately general processing residues.

what: What this function does NOT include
title: reportProcessingResiduesForestry — reportProcessingResiduesForestry
text: Logging residues (branches, tops): already MAgPIE woodfuel (v73_prod_residues) Post-consumer waste wood (demolition, furniture): excluded consistency carbonLTS, tracks CO2 HWP decay using IPCC first-order decay (half-lives: sawnwood 30yr, construction 60yr, pulpwood 2yr). Adding end--life energy recovery require splitting HWP outflow burned vs decayed fractions — assumptions currently model. CO2 HWP decay already reported; energy burning waste wood tracked avoid parallel lifetime assumptions. Black liquor: burned internally pulp mills, available external energy

title: reportProcessingResiduesForestry — reportProcessingResiduesForestry
text: FAO/UNECE conversion factors sawnwood recovery rates Mantau, U. (2012). Wood flows Europe (EU27). UNECE/FAO Discussion Paper 51 (DP-51). Oswalt et al. (2019). Forest Resources United States. USDA. Churkina et al. (2020). Buildings global carbon sink. Nature Sustainability.
